text: High above the world, nestled amongst the softest clouds, floated Cloud Castle. Home to the kind and curious Princess Everly. But Everly had a very unusual best friend. Not a fairy, not a talking animal of the forest below, but a tiny Tyrannosaurus Rex named Eli.
(Giggles) Come on, Eli! Catch me if you can!
(Stopping and turning) You’re getting faster, little one! Almost as fast as a cloud breeze!
Eli wasn't like the fearsome dinosaurs of old tales. He was gentle, playful, and surprisingly good at fetching lost hair ribbons.
You know, Eli, I was thinking… Cloud Castle is lovely, but sometimes I wish we could explore a little more.
I’ve heard stories from the wind spirits about a legendary Rainbow Waterfall. They say it shimmers with every color imaginable and tastes like pure sunshine!
I know, right? It sounds amazing! But it’s said to be hidden somewhere beyond the Whispering Mists. Papa says it’s too dangerous for little princesses.
(Smiling slightly) But… maybe we could have a little pretend adventure? Right here in the courtyard?
Okay! The courtyard… is now the treacherous Cloud Forest leading to the Whispering Mists! And that pile of fluffy clouds over there… is Mount Fluffernutter, the tallest peak we must climb!
Wait for me, silly! And remember, the ground is very… bouncy… and might… tickle your toes!
Oh dear! A hidden cloud trap! Luckily, Princess Everly is very agile!
(Laughing) Need a little boost, brave explorer?
You did it! You conquered Mount Fluffernutter! Now, beyond lies the Whispering Mists! We must be very quiet. Legend says the mists whisper secrets… and tickle noses!
(Whispering) Can you hear them, Eli? The secrets of the clouds… and maybe… just maybe… the way to the Rainbow Waterfall!
Even if we don’t find the real Rainbow Waterfall today, Eli… this pretend adventure was pretty wonderful, wasn't it?

And so, Princess Everly and her miniature T-Rex friend, Eli, continued their imaginative journey through the cloud courtyard, their laughter and soft roars echoing in the gentle breeze. For even in a castle made of clouds, the greatest adventures are often the ones you create together.
